degree_class: LM-9 R - Medical, Veterinary and Pharmaceutical Biotechnology

academic_requirements.summary: Applicants need a minimum three-year bachelor's degree or equivalent to be obtained by September 30, 2026. The programme is intended for graduates in biotechnology, pharmacy, industrial pharmacy, biological sciences, chemical sciences and technologies, technical health professions, or related fields with the required disciplinary background.
academic_requirements.prior_degree_or_diploma: A minimum three-year undergraduate degree (or equivalent) in Biotechnology, Pharmacy, Industrial Pharmacy, Biological Sciences, Chemical Sciences and Technologies, Technical Health Professions, or related fields.
academic_requirements.subject_requirements: Applicants must have proven competencies in Biology, Chemistry, Medicine, and Veterinary Science. The official call lists areas including physiology, biochemistry, molecular biology, pharmacology, human anatomy, genetics, microbiology, analytical and organic chemistry, pharmaceutical chemistry, general pathology, clinical microbiology, and veterinary pharmacology and pathology.
academic_requirements.gpa_or_country_requirements: No minimum GPA is stated. The degree must be eligible under the University of Padua country-specific entry-title requirements.

accepted_proofs: IELTS Academic or General Training with minimum score 6.0
Cambridge English B2 First with minimum score 173
Oxford Test of English with minimum score 126
Pearson PTE Academic with minimum score 65
Other certificates explicitly accepted on the official University of Padua language requirement page
exemption_rules: Applicants are exempt if the previous degree used as the entry qualification was completed entirely in English.
Applicants are exempt if they are native English speakers from one of the countries listed by the University of Padua.
Only certificates obtained no more than five years before the start date of the selection process are accepted.
language_requirements.deadline: August 25, 2026 for the EU and EU-equated foreign-qualification call.

application_deadline_eu.notes: This is the EU and EU-equated foreign-qualification call for Academic Year 2026/2027. Eligible non-EU applicants already residing in Italy follow this route if they meet the residence-permit conditions stated in the official call.

application_deadline_non_eu.notes: This call is for non-EU candidates residing outside Italy. The official programme factsheet also states that applications may be submitted only once per academic year and shows 30 places for this category.

required_documents.notes: The EU and EU-equated call asks for a valid identity document or passport.

required_documents.document_name: Academic transcript of records and bachelor's degree diploma
required_documents.notes: Upload the transcript with exams, grades, and grading scale, and the bachelor's diploma if already obtained.

required_documents.document_name: Official translations into Italian or English
required_documents.notes: Required if academic documents are issued in a language other than Italian or English.
Upload if already obtained. In the EU and EU-equated call, proof of B2 English must be submitted by August 25, 2026 if missing at application stage.
Required as part of the online application.

required_documents.document_name: Legalisation or Apostille or CIMEA Statement of Verification
required_documents.notes: Admitted candidates must submit one of these qualification-verification documents by October 31, 2026. A CIMEA Statement of Comparability may also be requested if needed.

entry_exam_or_test.applies_to: The official 2026/2027 EU and EU-equated call explicitly requires the test. The official programme factsheet for non-EU applicants residing abroad does not restate the full 2026/2027 test procedure in the source used here [uncertain].
entry_exam_or_test.dates: September 2, 2026 at 9:30 am local time (GMT+2). From August 28, 2026 the university publishes reporting instructions and room assignments on the official admission webpage.
entry_exam_or_test.scoring_or_threshold: The test is in English and has 60 multiple-choice questions to be completed in 90 minutes. Scoring is +1 for each correct answer, -0.1 for each incorrect answer, and 0 for unanswered questions. Candidates must score at least 25/60 to enter the ranking.

source_quotes.quote: Call One for non-EU students: 7 January 2026 – 7 March 2026
English language: B2 level (CEFR) or equivalent
On-site admission test, with a score of 25/60 or higher.

uncertainty_notes: The University of Padua states that English translations of admission documents are for information only; in case of disputes, the Italian version has legal validity.

For the non-EU abroad route, the deadline and place count were taken from the official apply.unipd.it programme factsheet because the public unipd.it source used here exposed the EU and EU-equated 2026/2027 call in full detail.
